sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 56

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 56:1
items.html:
(to the question) Tipat Odem [red drop]
Ima [mother] sows the redness in the newborn, which is the discernment of her MAN [Mayin Nukvin], being the discernment of GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] of the lower one that rose and were clarified out of the Klipot [shells], while the lower Hey is still in their Eynaim — that is, the force of the Tzimtzum [restriction] and the Dinim [judgments]. They are also called five Gevurot, being the discernment of BON and Gevurot. And the father sows the whiteness in the newborn, which is the discernment of the Lights that emerge upon the Zivug of the incorporation of the MAN and their Aviut [coarseness] within the Masach [screen] of Katnut [smallness] of AVI [Aba ve Ima]. These Lights are the drop of MAD [Mayin Duchrin, male waters], and are called five Hassadim. Thus, Ima's share in that Zivug is the discernment of the Masach of Katnut of her own embryo state, in which the Aviut of the MAN — the five Gevurot — is incorporated; and Aba's share is the extension of the upper Light, which strikes the Masach and the Aviut in Ima's Masach. From these two the newborn is built. (p. 787, Item 82)
